findings.description: REQ-REF-008 API endpoints (GET /api/v1/contents/{id}/references, GET /api/v1/contents/{id}/cited-by) lack authentication specification. Existing content routes use router-level Depends(verify_admin_key). Task 7.1 creates a new router but doesn't specify whether it requires admin auth or is public. Neither the spec nor the design addresses authentication for these read-only endpoints.
findings.resolution: Specify in REQ-REF-008: 'Endpoints require session cookie or X-Admin-Key authentication, consistent with existing content routes.' In Task 7.1, add: 'router = APIRouter(dependencies=[Depends(verify_admin_key)])' following src/api/content_routes.py pattern.

findings.description: MCP tool ingest_reference(reference_id) allows ad-hoc ingestion of any unresolved reference by ID. Since auto-ingest is disabled by default (reference_auto_ingest_enabled=False), this tool effectively bypasses the setting gate. A malicious or misconfigured MCP client could trigger unbounded ingestion by calling ingest_reference in a loop for all unresolved references.
findings.resolution: Decision: Option A — ingest_reference is intentionally independent of reference_auto_ingest_enabled. The setting gates unattended background auto-ingestion only. Ad-hoc MCP tool invocation requires explicit per-reference action and is not gated. Documented in REQ-REF-013 and MCP tool docstring.

findings.description: Design code shows store_references using on_conflict_do_nothing(constraint='uq_content_reference'), but this only handles references WITH external_id. URL-only references (external_id=NULL) use the partial unique index uq_content_reference_url, not the table constraint. The on_conflict_do_nothing call does not specify a fallback for the partial index path, so duplicate URL-only references would fail with IntegrityError instead of being silently skipped.
findings.resolution: Add a second conflict handling path for URL-only references: when ref.external_id is None, use on_conflict_do_nothing(index_elements=['source_content_id', 'external_url'], postgresql_where=text('external_id IS NULL')). Or use a try/except IntegrityError as fallback.

findings.description: The design shows resolve_references handler as an async function using get_db() context manager. However, the existing custom worker in src/queue/worker.py processes jobs synchronously within a thread pool — handlers are called inside asyncio.to_thread() or similar. The handler signature and DB session management must match the worker's actual invocation pattern.
findings.resolution: Verify whether the custom worker calls handlers with async or sync pattern. If sync, the resolve_references handler should be sync and use get_db() directly. If async, it should use asyncio to_thread for DB calls. Check src/queue/worker.py handler dispatch pattern before implementation.

findings.description: REQ-REF-002 lists 'Internal content URLs (matching the application's own URL patterns)' as a reference type to extract, but no regex pattern, normalization logic, or scenario covers this. The design's REFERENCE_PATTERNS dict only includes arXiv, DOI, and S2. Internal URL matching requires knowing the app's base URL which varies by deployment.
findings.resolution: Either: (1) remove 'internal content URLs' from REQ-REF-002 scope and note it as a future enhancement, or (2) add a REFERENCE_PATTERNS entry for internal URLs using a configurable base URL pattern from Settings. Add a scenario covering internal URL detection.

findings.description: The design's extract_from_content code calls content.markdown_content directly without null check on line 144. Task 2.6 says 'Handles None/empty markdown_content gracefully' but the design pseudocode would raise AttributeError on NoneType if markdown_content is None, since re.finditer(pattern, None) throws TypeError.
findings.resolution: Add early return in the design pseudocode: 'if not content.markdown_content: return []' before the regex scan loop. This matches the scenario 'Content with empty markdown_content' added in iteration 1.

findings.description: Task 5.3 wires hooks into 7+ orchestrator functions (ingest_gmail, ingest_rss, ingest_youtube, etc.). This is a large surface area for a cross-cutting concern. If a new ingestion source is added later (e.g., add-arxiv-ingest), the developer must remember to add the hook call. This violates the open-closed principle.
findings.resolution: Consider an alternative: add the hook in a centralized post-ingestion point rather than in each orchestrator function. For example, if the queue worker's process_content entrypoint calls extraction, all sources benefit automatically. If per-function hooks are preferred, add a CLAUDE.md gotcha: 'New ingestion orchestrator functions MUST call on_content_ingested() from src/services/reference_hook after content persist.'

findings.description: The design's extract_from_content loads ALL chunks for a content item via db.query(DocumentChunk).filter(...).all(). For long documents with hundreds of chunks, this loads all chunk text into memory. The chunks are only needed for offset matching — their full text isn't required.
findings.resolution: Use a projection query: only load id, chunk_index, and len(chunk_text) (or a stored chunk_text_length column). Alternatively, accept current approach since chunk counts are typically <50 per document and text is already in memory from the indexing step.

findings.description: REQ-REF-008 specifies API response should include 'target content summary (if resolved)' but doesn't define what 'summary' means — is it the content title? First N characters of markdown? A summary record? The Pydantic schema (ReferenceResponse, Task 7.4) doesn't specify these fields.
findings.resolution: Define explicitly: resolved references include target_content_id, target_title (Content.title), target_source_type, and target_source_url. Do NOT include full markdown_content or summary text in the reference response to avoid N+1 or payload bloat.

findings.description: Task 1.5 updates the Content SQLAlchemy model to use JSONB instead of JSON for metadata_json. This is a model-level change that affects ALL code touching Content.metadata_json — not just reference-related code. Any query using Content.metadata_json comparisons or serialization could behave differently with JSONB (e.g., key ordering, duplicate key handling). This is a cross-cutting change bundled into a feature-specific migration.
findings.resolution: This is acceptable since the JSON→JSONB migration is idempotent and JSONB is strictly superior for queries. Note in the task that this change is intentionally broader than the reference feature — it benefits all metadata_json queries. Add a regression test verifying existing metadata_json reads/writes work after the type change.

findings.description: The SUPPLEMENTS bidirectional reference creation (design.md, Scholar↔arXiv link) creates two rows with the same external_id and external_id_type but different source_content_id values. The uq_content_reference constraint is on (source_content_id, external_id, external_id_type), so both rows have unique combinations. However, if the arXiv→Scholar row also sets external_id=arxiv_id with external_id_type=ARXIV, it semantically says 'arXiv paper references this arXiv ID' which is self-referential and confusing.
findings.resolution: For the reverse direction (arXiv→Scholar), use a different identifier if available (e.g., external_id=scholar_s2_paper_id, external_id_type=S2) or set external_id=None with only external_url. This makes the bidirectional rows semantically clearer. Alternatively, document that both rows use the same arXiv ID by convention and this is expected.

findings.description: REQ-REF-009 specifies CLI options --after DATE and --before DATE but doesn't specify the date format. Existing CLI commands use ISO 8601 (YYYY-MM-DD). The spec should be consistent.
findings.resolution: Add 'ISO 8601 date format (YYYY-MM-DD)' to the option descriptions.

findings.description: Design decision 'Why anchor to DocumentChunk instead of text snippet?' references 'start_char/end_char give precise structural location' but the design itself acknowledges these fields are never populated. The decision rationale contradicts the implementation reality.
findings.resolution: Update the design decision text: remove reference to start_char/end_char precision, or reframe as 'chunk_index and section_path give structural location; start_char/end_char are available for future precision improvements.'

findings.description: REQ-REF-008 API endpoints don't specify pagination parameters. Existing API routes in this codebase use page/page_size query parameters with standard pagination metadata in responses.
findings.resolution: Add pagination parameters to REQ-REF-008: 'Endpoints accept page (default 1) and page_size (default 20) query parameters, returning standard pagination metadata.'

findings.description: The existing ReferenceExtractor in src/ingestion/reference_extractor.py has a close() method for the Scholar service. After migration to src/services/, the class will have both the new stateless extraction methods AND the old stateful Scholar-service-based methods. This mixed responsibility may confuse future contributors.
findings.resolution: After migration, consider splitting into two classes: ReferenceExtractor (stateless, extraction + store) and ScholarReferenceIngester (stateful, wraps Scholar service for scholar-refs CLI). The ingester can live alongside or compose the extractor. Not blocking — can be addressed during implementation if the mixed class becomes unwieldy.
